perturbation.original_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title, and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall be transferred to the Recipient upon disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original statement that the disclosing party retains all rights to the confidential information. By stating that rights are 'transferred to the Recipient,' we create an inconsistency in ownership, this introduces an internal contradiction and it affects the enforcement of the rest of the clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that all rights to the confidential information are transferred to the recipient, directly contradicting the original text, which stated that the disclosing party retains all rights. The contradicted text reinforces that the disclosing party retains rights by stating that the recipient is not granted rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets. Thus, the modified text creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the ownership and rights associated with the confidential information, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confident ial Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain, other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient; or (b) at any time rightfully received from a third party which had the right to and transmits it to the Rec ipient without any obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain; or (b) at any time rightfully received from a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient' from (a), we now state that even if the Recipient causes the information to become public, the confidentiality obligations no longer apply. Similarly, by removing 'without any obligation of confidentiality' from (b) we eliminate the requirement that the third party had no obligation of confidentiality for the exception to apply. This creates an in-text contradiction and opens up loopholes, greatly weakening the NDA's protection of confidential information, creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded i t by law, and in addition may recover all reasonable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies. If the Confidential Information is sought by any third party, including by way of subpoena or other court process, the Recipient shall inform the Disclosing Party of the request in sufficient time to permit the Disclosing Party to object to and, if necessary, seek court intervention to prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations. By removing 'other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient' and 'without any obligation of confidentiality', the changed text now allows the Recipient to breach the agreement and disclose confidential information without consequence, or receive confidential information from a third party that has no obligation of confidentiality without consequence. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the Disclosing Party is entitled to legal and equitable remedies in the event of a breach by the Recipient.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded i t by law, and in addition may recover all reasonable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Recipient *chooses* to breach this Agreement, the Disclosing Party's remedies are limited to a written apology.
perturbation.explanation: This fundamentally alters the remedies available to the Disclosing Party in case of a breach. Originally, the Disclosing Party had access to 'all legal and equitable remedies.' Now, those are limited to a written apology. This contradiction could render the agreement unenforceable because the remedies are so limited compared to general legal principles. This perturbation introduces a direct in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be gover ned in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado . This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties. This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement betwee n the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Disclosing Party to pursue all legal and equitable remedies in the event of a breach. The changed text limits the remedies to a written apology if the Recipient *chooses* to breach the agreement. This change directly contradicts the general principle that the agreement's enforceability is governed by the law of Colorado as outlined in the contradicted text, essentially rendering the agreement unenforceable as the original text stipulated that all legal and equitable remedies were available in the event of a breach. The change is inconsistent with established legal remedies, violating the assumption of enforceability within Colorado law.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement can be amended orally by either party at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Agreement can only be amended in writing, signed by both parties. By changing it to allow for oral amendments by either party, we create a direct contradiction. This creates an internal contradiction and uncertainty, as it becomes unclear how the agreement can be modified validly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement betwee n the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. Disclosing Party: Company: _______________________ Name: __________________________ Role: ___________________________ Phone Number: __________________ Signature: ___________________________ ____ The Recipient : Company: _____ Pitch Deck Fire ______ Name: _______ Stacie Shaw __________ Role: _________ Founder ___________ Phone Number: __ _214-770-9216 ____ Signature: _______________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text allows for oral amendments by either party. This creates a direct conflict. The Contradicted Text presents the agreement as a signed document, implying that changes should also be formal and signed. The change creates an internal contradiction, as it becomes unclear how the agreement can be validly modified. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
